verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim Dominator, Dominus exercituum,  auferet a Jerusalem et a Juda  validum et fortem,  omne robur panis, et omne robor aquæ ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי֩ הִנֵּ֨ה הָאָד֜וֹן יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֗וֹת מֵסִ֤יר מִירוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ וּמִ֣יהוּדָ֔ה מַשְׁעֵ֖ן וּמַשְׁעֵנָ֑ה כֹּ֚ל מִשְׁעַן־לֶ֔חֶם וְכֹ֖ל מִשְׁעַן־מָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: For, behold, the Lord, the Lord of hosts, doth take away from Jerusalem and from Judah the stay and the staff, the whole stay of bread, and the whole stay of water,

verses.latin_vulgate: fortem, et virum bellatorem,  judicem, et prophetam, et ariolum, et senem ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גִּבּ֖וֹר וְאִ֣ישׁ מִלְחָמָ֑ה שׁוֹפֵ֥ט וְנָבִ֖יא וְקֹסֵ֥ם וְזָקֵֽן׃
verses.king_james: The mighty man, and the man of war, the judge, and the prophet, and the prudent, and the ancient,

verses.latin_vulgate: principem super quinquaginta, et honorabilem vultu et consiliarium,  et sapientem de architectis, et prudentem eloquii mystici.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׂר־חֲמִשִּׁ֖ים וּנְשׂ֣וּא פָנִ֑ים וְיוֹעֵ֛ץ וַחֲכַ֥ם חֲרָשִׁ֖ים וּנְב֥וֹן לָֽחַשׁ׃
verses.king_james: The captain of fifty, and the honourable man, and the counsellor, and the cunning artificer, and the eloquent orator.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dabo pueros principes eorum,  et effeminati dominabuntur eis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָתַתִּ֥י נְעָרִ֖ים שָׂרֵיהֶ֑ם וְתַעֲלוּלִ֖ים יִמְשְׁלוּ־בָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And I will give children to be their princes, and babes shall rule over them.

verses.latin_vulgate: et irruet populus, vir ad virum,  et unusquisque ad proximum suum ;  tumultuabitur puer contra senem,  et ignobilis contra nobilem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנִגַּ֣שׂ הָעָ֔ם אִ֥ישׁ בְּאִ֖ישׁ וְאִ֣ישׁ בְּרֵעֵ֑הוּ יִרְהֲב֗וּ הַנַּ֙עַר֙ בַּזָּקֵ֔ן וְהַנִּקְלֶ֖ה בַּנִּכְבָּֽד׃
verses.king_james: And the people shall be oppressed, every one by another, and every one by his neighbour: the child shall behave himself proudly against the ancient, and the base against the honourable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Apprehendet enim vir fratrem suum,  domesticum patris sui :  Vestimentum tibi est, princeps esto noster,  ruina autem hæc sub manu tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־יִתְפֹּ֨שׂ אִ֤ישׁ בְּאָחִיו֙ בֵּ֣ית אָבִ֔יו שִׂמְלָ֣ה לְכָ֔ה קָצִ֖ין תִּֽהְיֶה־לָּ֑נוּ וְהַמַּכְשֵׁלָ֥ה הַזֹּ֖את תַּ֥חַת יָדֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: When a man shall take hold of his brother of the house of his father, saying, Thou hast clothing, be thou our ruler, and let this ruin be under thy hand:

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondebit in die illa, dicens :  Non sum medicus,  et in domo mea non est panis neque vestimentum :  nolite constituere me principem populi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשָּׂא֩ בַיּ֨וֹם הַה֤וּא ׀ לֵאמֹר֙ לֹא־אֶהְיֶ֣ה חֹבֵ֔שׁ וּבְבֵיתִ֕י אֵ֥ין לֶ֖חֶם וְאֵ֣ין שִׂמְלָ֑ה לֹ֥א תְשִׂימֻ֖נִי קְצִ֥ין עָֽם׃
verses.king_james: In that day shall he swear, saying, I will not be an healer; for in my house is neither bread nor clothing: make me not a ruler of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ruit enim Jerusalem, et Judas concidit,  quia lingua eorum et adinventiones eorum contra Dominum,  ut provocarent oculos majestatis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י כָֽשְׁלָה֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וִיהוּדָ֖ה נָפָ֑ל כִּֽי־לְשׁוֹנָ֤ם וּמַֽעַלְלֵיהֶם֙ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֔ה לַמְר֖וֹת עֵנֵ֥י כְבוֹדֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For Jerusalem is ruined, and Judah is fallen: because their tongue and their doings are against the Lord, to provoke the eyes of his glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Agnitio vultus eorum respondit eis ;  et peccatum suum quasi Sodoma prædicaverunt, nec absconderunt.  Væ animæ eorum, quoniam reddita sunt eis mala !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַכָּרַ֤ת פְּנֵיהֶם֙ עָ֣נְתָה בָּ֔ם וְחַטָּאתָ֛ם כִּסְדֹ֥ם הִגִּ֖ידוּ לֹ֣א כִחֵ֑דוּ א֣וֹי לְנַפְשָׁ֔ם כִּי־גָמְל֥וּ לָהֶ֖ם רָעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The shew of their countenance doth witness against them; and they declare their sin as Sodom, they hide it not. Woe unto their soul! for they have rewarded evil unto themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicite justo quoniam bene,  quoniam fructum adinventionum suarum comedet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִמְר֥וּ צַדִּ֖יק כִּי־ט֑וֹב כִּֽי־פְרִ֥י מַעַלְלֵיהֶ֖ם יֹאכֵֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Say ye to the righteous, that it shall be well with him: for they shall eat the fruit of their doings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ impio in malum !  retributio enim manuum ejus fiet ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֖וֹי לְרָשָׁ֣ע רָ֑ע כִּֽי־גְמ֥וּל יָדָ֖יו יֵעָ֥שֶׂה לּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto the wicked! it shall be ill with him: for the reward of his hands shall be given him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Populum meum exactores sui spoliaverunt,  et mulieres dominatæ sunt eis.  Popule meus, qui te beatum dicunt, ipsi te decipiunt,  et viam gressuum tuorum dissipant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַמִּי֙ נֹגְשָׂ֣יו מְעוֹלֵ֔ל וְנָשִׁ֖ים מָ֣שְׁלוּ ב֑וֹ עַמִּי֙ מְאַשְּׁרֶ֣יךָ מַתְעִ֔ים וְדֶ֥רֶךְ אֹרְחֹתֶ֖יךָ בִּלֵּֽעוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: As for my people, children are their oppressors, and women rule over them. O my people, they which lead thee cause thee to err, and destroy the way of thy paths.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stat ad judicandum Dominus,  et stat ad judicandos populos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִצָּ֥ב לָרִ֖יב יְהֹוָ֑ה וְעֹמֵ֖ד לָדִ֥ין עַמִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: The Lord standeth up to plead, and standeth to judge the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus ad judicium veniet  cum senibus populi sui, et principibus ejus ;  vos enim depasti estis vineam,  et rapina pauperis in domo vestra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָה֙ בְּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט יָב֔וֹא עִם־זִקְנֵ֥י עַמּ֖וֹ וְשָׂרָ֑יו וְאַתֶּם֙ בִּעַרְתֶּ֣ם הַכֶּ֔רֶם גְּזֵלַ֥ת הֶעָנִ֖י בְּבָתֵּיכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: The Lord will enter into judgment with the ancients of his people, and the princes thereof: for ye have eaten up the vineyard; the spoil of the poor is in your houses.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare atteritis populum meum,  et facies pauperum commolitis ?  dicit Dominus Deus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (מלכם) [מַה־לָּכֶם֙] תְּדַכְּא֣וּ עַמִּ֔י וּפְנֵ֥י עֲנִיִּ֖ים תִּטְחָ֑נוּ נְאֻם־אֲדֹנָ֥י יֱהֹוִ֖ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: What mean ye that ye beat my people to pieces, and grind the faces of the poor? saith the Lord God of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus :  Pro eo quod elevatæ sunt filiæ Sion,  et ambulaverunt extento collo,  et nutibus oculorum ibant,  et plaudebant, ambulabant pedibus suis,  et composito gradu incedebant ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְהֹוָ֗ה יַ֚עַן כִּ֤י גָֽבְהוּ֙ בְּנ֣וֹת צִיּ֔וֹן וַתֵּלַ֙כְנָה֙ (נטוות) [נְטוּי֣וֹת] גָּר֔וֹן וּֽמְשַׂקְּר֖וֹת עֵינָ֑יִם הָל֤וֹךְ וְטָפֹף֙ תֵּלַ֔כְנָה וּבְרַגְלֵיהֶ֖ם תְּעַכַּֽסְנָה׃
verses.king_james: Moreover the Lord saith, Because the daughters of Zion are haughty, and walk with stretched forth necks and wanton eyes, walking and mincing as they go, and making a tinkling with their feet:

verses.latin_vulgate: decalvabit Dominus verticem filiarum Sion,  et Dominus crinem earum nudabit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשִׂפַּ֣ח אֲדֹנָ֔י קׇדְקֹ֖ד בְּנ֣וֹת צִיּ֑וֹן וַיהֹוָ֖ה פׇּתְהֵ֥ן יְעָרֶֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Therefore the Lord will smite with a scab the crown of the head of the daughters of Zion, and the Lord will discover their secret parts.

verses.latin_vulgate: In die illa auferet Dominus ornamentum calceamentum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֨וֹם הַה֜וּא יָסִ֣יר אֲדֹנָ֗י אֵ֣ת תִּפְאֶ֧רֶת הָעֲכָסִ֛ים וְהַשְּׁבִיסִ֖ים וְהַשַּׂהֲרֹנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: In that day the Lord will take away the bravery of their tinkling ornaments about their feet, and their cauls, and their round tires like the moon,

verses.latin_vulgate: et lunulas, et torques,  et monilia, et armillas, et mitras,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַנְּטִפ֥וֹת וְהַשֵּׁיר֖וֹת וְהָרְעָלֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: The chains, and the bracelets, and the mufflers,

verses.latin_vulgate: et discriminalia, et periscelidas, et murenulas,  et olfactoriola, et inaures,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַפְּאֵרִ֤ים וְהַצְּעָדוֹת֙ וְהַקִּשֻּׁרִ֔ים וּבָתֵּ֥י הַנֶּ֖פֶשׁ וְהַלְּחָשִֽׁים׃
verses.king_james: The bonnets, and the ornaments of the legs, and the headbands, and the tablets, and the earrings,

verses.latin_vulgate: et annulos, et gemmas in fronte pendentes,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַטַּבָּע֖וֹת וְנִזְמֵ֥י הָאָֽף׃
verses.king_james: The rings, and nose jewels,

verses.latin_vulgate: et mutatoria, et palliola,  et linteamina, et acus,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַמַּֽחֲלָצוֹת֙ וְהַמַּ֣עֲטָפ֔וֹת וְהַמִּטְפָּח֖וֹת וְהָחֲרִיטִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The changeable suits of apparel, and the mantles, and the wimples, and the crisping pins,

verses.latin_vulgate: et specula, et sindones,  et vittas, et theristra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַגִּלְיֹנִים֙ וְהַסְּדִינִ֔ים וְהַצְּנִיפ֖וֹת וְהָרְדִידִֽים׃
verses.king_james: The glasses, and the fine linen, and the hoods, and the vails.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit pro suavi odore fœtor,  et pro zona funiculus,  et pro crispanti crine calvitium,  et pro fascia pectorali cilicium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֩ תַ֨חַת בֹּ֜שֶׂם מַ֣ק יִֽהְיֶ֗ה וְתַ֨חַת חֲגוֹרָ֤ה נִקְפָּה֙ וְתַ֨חַת מַעֲשֶׂ֤ה מִקְשֶׁה֙ קׇרְחָ֔ה וְתַ֥חַת פְּתִיגִ֖יל מַחֲגֹ֣רֶת שָׂ֑ק כִּי־תַ֖חַת יֹֽפִי׃
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass, that instead of sweet smell there shall be stink; and instead of a girdle a rent; and instead of well set hair baldness; and instead of a stomacher a girding of sackcloth; and burning instead of beauty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pulcherrimi quoque viri tui gladio cadent,  et fortes tui in prælio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְתַ֖יִךְ בַּחֶ֣רֶב יִפֹּ֑לוּ וּגְבוּרָתֵ֖ךְ בַּמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Thy men shall fall by the sword, and thy mighty in the war.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mœrebunt atque lugebunt portæ ejus,  et desolata in terra sedebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָנ֥וּ וְאָבְל֖וּ פְּתָחֶ֑יהָ וְנִקָּ֖תָה לָאָ֥רֶץ תֵּשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: And her gates shall lament and mourn; and she being desolate shall sit upon the ground.
